codeReview.summary: The feature correctly split maximize/focus actors by window label, but it still leaves blocking stale-ownership risk for same-label close/reopen cycles because cached actors are never torn down or refreshed and the added tests only prove different labels get different actors.

issues.description: windowActors is a never-evicted map keyed only by label, and each actor captures Window.getCurrent() when first created. If a window closes and a new renderer reopens under the same label, getWindowMaximizedActor() reuses the old actor still bound to the disposed native window, so maximize ownership can stay stale across reopen cycles.

The focus actor cache is also label-keyed with no close/reopen teardown path. A reopened renderer that reuses a previous label receives the existing actor and its old event-subscription lifecycle instead of a fresh ownership instance, so the required reopen freshness contract is not actually implemented.

The added regression tests cover only different labels (main vs main-1). They do not simulate closing and reopening a window under the same label, so they do not verify the milestone requirement that open-close-reopen sequences get fresh control ownership and lifecycle state.

summary: Fail: same-label reopen freshness remains unresolved. The feature localizes actors per label, but both maximize and focus caches are immortal label-only maps, so a reopened window can still inherit stale actor ownership; the tests never cover that failure mode.
